titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh, Photograph Albums and Photographic Postcards of Minnesota, Montana, North Dakota, and Wisconsin
Welsh (Alvina Sophia Alm), Photograph Albums and Photographic Postcards of Minnesota, Montana, North Dakota, and Wisconsin
titlestmt.author: Ashley Cale and Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 16332098.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 22, 2025 at 23:01

did.unittitle: Photograph albums and photographic postcards of Minnesota, Montana, North Dakota, and Wisconsin

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Photograph albums and photographic postcards created, collected, and compiled by Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh that document the social and cultural life of the Alm and Welsh families as well as their network of friends and acquaintances chiefly in Minnesota, Montana, North Dakota, and Wisconsin, approximately 1908-1930, with the bulk of material, approximately 1913-1920. The collection includes more than 240 photographic postcards as well as a photograph album with 127 photographs and 41 photographic prints from a photograph album dismantled before its acquisition by the library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh, Photograph Albums and Photographic Postcards of Minnesota, Montana, North Dakota, and Wisconsin, is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Langdon Manor Books, LLC, on the Wiliam Robertson Coe Fund No. 3, 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Photograph Albums, approximately 1913-1920. II. Photographic Postcards, approximately 1908-1930.

bioghist.head: Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh (1893-1974)
bioghist.p: Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh was a schoolteacher in eastern Montana. She was born in Alden, Wisconsin, to second-generation Norwegian American Edward Christensen Alm (1857-1919) and Anna Larsdatter Gilbertson Alm (1862-1935), who immigrated from Norway during childhood, with siblings including an older brother Levi Carl Alm (1891-1966) and younger siblings Maurice Sigfred Alm (1896-1984) and Gena Alm Larson (1897-1977).

Welsh attended a school in Amery, Wisconsin, and in June-August 1912 completed a summer session course related to teacher training at the Superior Normal School, Superior, Wisconsin. By 1913, she was teaching at Yates, Montana, and later at nearby Fallon.

In June 1918, she ceased teaching and married farmer Raleigh Harrison Welsh (1889-1970), son of Albert Welsh (1846-1934) and Zeete Angelina Mowry Welsh (1849-1903), who established a homestead in Wibaux County, Montana, and one of seven siblings that survived infancy. They engaged in farming at the Welsh family homestead in Wibaux County and had a son, Duane Curtis Welsh (1924-2019). Around 1944-1946, the couple retired and settled in Wibaux. Both died in hospitals in Beach, North Dakota.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh, Photograph Albums and Photographic Postcards of Minnesota, Montana, North Dakota, and Wisconsin. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual. This guide derives from a detailed examination of each print. This includes an inital arrangement by Ashley Cale as well as a review by Matthew Daniel Mason, who also drafted the front matter to this guide.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph albums and photographic postcards created, collected, and compiled by Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh that document the social and cultural life of the Alm and Welsh families as well as their network of friends and acquaintances chiefly in Minnesota, Montana, North Dakota, and Wisconsin, approximately 1908-1930. The collection includes more than 240 photographic postcards as well as a photograph album with 127 photographs and 41 photographic prints from a photograph album dismantled before its acquisition by the library.

Images in the albums and photographic postcards document informal interior and exterior portraits as well as commercial studio portraits of Welsh and others, including examples of persons wearing traditional Norwegian dress and crossdressing, as well as photographs related to logging, farming, and ranching activities including several views with agricultural machinery planting and harvesting crops. Social and recreational activities include excursions, picnics, and family gatherings. The collection also includes a group portrait of Native American children, women, and men, likely Ojibwe or Menominee, probably at a logging or maple-sugaring camp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I consists of photograph albums compiled by Alvina S. Alm Welsh, approximately 1913-1920, including an extant album and photographic prints from a dismantled album.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into two subseries: Album and Photographic Prints from a Dismantled Album.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph album compiled by Alvina S. Alm Welsh, approximately 1913-1920, with 127 photographic prints, 15.3 x 20.4 cm and smaller, that documents the social and cultural life of the Alm and Welsh families in Minnesota, Montana, North Dakota, and Wisconsin. Images document recreational activities, logging, farming, and ranching activities (including several views with agricultural machinery), local excursions, domestic work, hunting and fishing expeditions, schoolhouses, and automobiles.

Manuscript notations by an unidentified person identify individuals and locations as well as provide captions for many images, although some inscriptions and photographs exhibit fading or have become illegible. Several prints are damaged or dismounted from album leaves. Several images represented in the album also appear in the photographic postcards in the collection.

Individuals identified in the album include a toddler Stanley Lamont and members of the Gilbert and Wickersham families as well as many others identified by their forenames including Alice, Cora, Edna, Floyd, Gena, Irving, Ivan, Levi, Louise, Mabel, Maurice, Mike, and Olaf.

did.unittitle: Photographic Prints from a Dismantled Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographic prints from a photograph album compiled by Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh, approximately 1913-1920, and dismantled before acquisition by the library, which document the social and cultural life of Welsh and her friends. This material consists of forty-one photographic prints with many images duplicating prints in the extant album in the collection.

Images include interior, exterior, and studio portraits of Welsh and others, with many photographs related to a group identified as the Sucker Branch Choir as well as documentation of recreational activities including wading in a lake. A group of photographs documents a crew of the Northern Pacific Railway Company repairing railway tracks with a work train attached to a Baldwin E-2 locomotive (Number 383).

did.physdesc: 3 photographic prints, 14 x 8 cm and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two studio portraits of Welsh

did.unittitle: Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh and others cross-dressing
did.physdesc: 5 photographic prints, 11 x 6.8 cm and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior portraits of Welsh and other women and men cross-dressing

did.unittitle: Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh in a classroom

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interior portraits of Welsh posed sitting at a desk and teaching an adult man in a classroom

did.unittitle: Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh with another woman
did.physdesc: 11 photographic prints, 15.4 x 9.5 cm and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discrete exterior portraits of Welsh with another woman; the same woman does not appear in each image

did.unittitle: Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh with groups
did.physdesc: 7 photographic prints, 6.6 x 11.2 cm and smaller, and 1 photographic postcard, 13.8 x 8.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior portraits of Welsh with groups of women and men consisting of a set of six prints including a photographic postcard that documents a group of five women and three men that formed the Sucker Branch Choir as well as two discrete views of groups of women wading and floating in a lake

did.physdesc: 2 photographic postcards, trimmed, 12.9 x 8.7 cm and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Studio portraits of two women identified as Effie and Gena (likely Gena Alm Larson; one postcard by Kegel Photo Parlors at 404 Wabasha Street, Saint Paul, Minnesota, which was one of multiple studio locations operated in Minneapolis and Saint Paul, Minnesota, by Clayton Kregel (1880-1933) that offered a 10-minute photographic postcard service

did.physdesc: 7 photographic prints, 11.2 x 6.6 cm and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images include informal portraits of couples and groups

did.unittitle: Northern Pacific Railroad Company Railway repair
did.physdesc: 3 photographic prints, 7.7 x 10.1 cm and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II consists of 243 photographic postcards created and collected by Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh, approximately 1900-1930, that document the cultural and social life of the Alm and Welsh families as well as their network of family, friends, and acquaintances. Many postcards include correspondence and manuscript notations on rectos and versos. Some images documented in the photographic postcards also appear in the complete photograph album in the collection. There are also two printed picture postcards related to Superior, Wisconsin.
Photographs of social activities include weddings, picnics, family gatherings, and school sports teams.

Images of agricultural activities include planting and harvesting crops, building barns, and herding cattle for the market. The collection includes a group portrait of Native American children, women, and men, likely Ojibwe or Menominee persons, probably at a logging or maple sugaring camp.

Informal interior and exterior portraits as well as commercial studio portraits document Welsh and others, which also includes examples of individuals wearing traditional Norwegian dress and crossdressing.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into three subseries: Portraits of Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh, Portraits of Others, and Places.

did.unittitle: Portraits of Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two headings: Studio Portraits and Informal Portraits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes discrete portraits of Welsh with one other woman, although none of the women are identified

did.physdesc: 2 photographic postcards, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 1 photographic postcard, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

did.unittitle: Welsh with groups of men and women
did.physdesc: 4 photographic postcards, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 2 photographic postcards, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes duplicate prints

did.unittitle: Welsh at Amery Public School, Amery, Wisconsin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interior group portraits of students at Amery Public School, Amery, Wisconsin, that include Welsh in a portrait of freshmen and sophomore students (Welsh identified in the first row, fifth from the left) and a portrait of a school assembly (Welsh identified in the fifth row, third from the front/left)

did.unittitle: Welsh farmhouse and homestead at Wibaux, Montana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Views of the Burton Uri Welsh and Catherine Mae Mengel Welsh farmhouse and homestead at Wibaux, Montana, approximately 1913-1918, where prior to her marriage, Alvina Sophia Alm Welsh boarded while teaching in Yates; two images document Welsh with Catherine Welsh as well as one with the son of the latter Russell William Welsh; a group portrait at the site documents the meeting of an unidentified club, approximately 1912; there is also a view of threshing machinery in operation

did.unittitle: Welsh and students at Fallon, Montana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Group portraits of students, including one with Welsh, at Fallon, Montana

did.unittitle: Welsh and others at the Minnesota State Prison

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Informal portrait, likely by Maurice Alm, that documents Welsh with Mabel Marie Sistad Alm and likely Ole K. Sistad, identified elsewhere as Olaf; a detail from the image also appears in the extant photograph album

did.physdesc: 2 photographic postcards, 8.8 x 13.8 cm, and 1 photographic postcard, 13.8 x 8.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image of Alvina and others in recreational settings

did.unittitle: Welsh with a rifle on horseback at Fallon, Montana

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under four headings: Studio Portraits, Interior Portraits, Exterior Portraits, and Portraits Related to Activities.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two headings: Identified Persons and Unidentified Persons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Group studio portraits of the football team in 1913 and the women's basketball team, which also were state champions, in 1914 from Casselton, North Dakota

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mass-produced photographic postcard by Charles Olonzo Welch (1878-1919), a photographer in Beach, North Dakota, that documents Philomena Musil Faust (1909-1992), also known as Mrs. William Edward Faust, and the aftermath of frostbite injuries that led to the amputation of most of her hands following her experience at seven years old surviving a blizzard at Beach, North Dakota, January 11-12, 1917

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Studio portrait of John B. Helmeid (1883-1969), a carpenter living in Blanchardville, Wisconsin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Studio portrait of Reuben Cleveland Marks (1888-1965)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Studio portrait of William Henry Shay (1891-1980), a newspaper editor and postmaster at Somerset, Wisconsin

did.unittitle: Welsh, Earl M., and Eugene Lawrence Welsh

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Studio portrait of Earl M. Welsh (1909-1917) and Eugene Lawrence Welsh (1907-1977)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Studio portrait of a girl

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portraits of a girl and young women wearing white dresses, with the portraits of the young women likely related to graduation from high school

did.physdesc: 7 photographic postcards, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 1 photographic postcard, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discrete studio portraits of different women by themselves

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes postcard by the Post Card Shop, 7 Sixth Street, Minneapolis, Minnesota

did.unittitle: Couples, one woman and one man

did.physdesc: 9 photographic postcards, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 1 photographic postcard, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images include a couple wearing graduation gowns and another with the caption: Portland, Oregon

did.physdesc: 5 photographic postcards, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 1 photographic postcard, 8.8. x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portrait of groups posed in tableaus that include Western and cowboy dress, guns, and playing cards

did.physdesc: 3 photographic postcards, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 1 photographic postcard, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a group of four women wearing traditional Norwegian dress including bunads, skauts, and other accessories

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two headings: Identified Persons and Unidentified Persons and Still Lifes

did.physdesc: 1 photographic postcard, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 1 photographic postcard, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Postmortem portrait of Andrew Egenes (1885-1914), partially visible in the coffin, as well as related funeral decorations in a church at Deronda, Wisconsin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Informal portrait of a woman operating a telephone switchboard

did.unittitle: Classroom with dozens of students and three teachers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Postcard addressed to Annie Gilbertson Alm as Mrs. Ed Alm

did.physdesc: 2 photographic postcards, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 1 photographic postcard, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discrete images of funeral decorations comprised of flowers and studio portraits of the deceased persons

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two headings: Identified Persons and Locations and Unidentified Persons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image on both postcards that documents a group of school children and their teacher; correspondence on verso by Mildred [No Surname], the school teacher in the image

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence on verso by Marion [No Surname] describes the image

did.unittitle: Osceola High School men's basketball team

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a group portrait of a football team or sports club as well as a view of spectators at a football game; correspondence on postcards by "Mike" from Northfield, Minnesota; alternative identification may be Carleton College

did.physdesc: 1 photographic postcard, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 2 photographic postcards, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images include discrete portraits of a woman with a collie and a horse

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images include discrete portraits of a man posed standing with two motorcycles, another man with a horse, and a man holding a coronet as well as a man posed with a wolf (likely a taxidermy subject) in a trap with a printed attribution on verso to photographer L. A. (Lewis Alvin) Foster (1866-1938), Glendive, Montana, which the correspondent crossed out and wrote his own name, J. L. Brown, Fallon, Montana, in September 1915

did.physdesc: 1 photographic postcard, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 1 photographic postcard, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image includes a domesticated rabbit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One postcard presents two discrete portraits of different couples

did.physdesc: 3 photographic postcards, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 1 photographic postcard, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images include discrete portraits of women including the cast of a performance of the song "San Antonio," which likely refers to the 1907 song by Egbert Van Alstyne (1882-1951); correspondence on versos describe images as well as a discussion of their photography

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images related to a group of at least two women and three men and a group portrait as well as a portrait of one of the men posed sitting at a military monument that includes a cannon and cannon balls

did.physdesc: 1 photographic postcard, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 3 photographic postcards, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Group of postcards that document a group of at least two women and two men at a lake with a team of two horses and a horse-drawn carriage

did.physdesc: 1 photographic postcards, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 9 photographic postcard, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images include a view at a construction site, likely of a barn

did.physdesc: 1 photographic postcard, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 1 photographic postcard, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes portraits of the team and a couple of players wearing uniforms with a monogram combining the letters A, H, and S

did.unittitle: Group of Native American children, women, and men

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Group portrait of Native American children, women, and men, likely Ojibwe or Menominee persons, probably at a logging or maple sugaring camp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes views of individual and groups with steam-powered tractors and threshing machinery

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under seven headings: Minnesota, Montana, North Dakota, Oregon, South Dakota, Washington (State), and Wisconsin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of the Siloa Lutheran Church

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of the arena at the Longfellow Zoological Gardens, a zoo and garden in Minnehaha neighborhood of Minneapolis, Minnesota, which operated from 1906 to 1934

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of the public school

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior portrait of a woman posed with an automobile, which includes a pennant for Fallon, Montana, at the BN Ranch

did.physdesc: 3 photographic postcards, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 5 photographic postcards, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes views of the Montana State Bank and railyard at Fallon as well as sites in the surrounding area including a ranch and the Terry Badlands with individuals gathering creeping cedar for church decorations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of views of the railroad depot from the town and the railway platform

did.physdesc: 1 photographic postcard, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 17 photographic postcards, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of views chiefly by L. A. (Lewis Alvin) Foster (1866-1938), a professional photographer based in Glendive, Montana, which include a bird's-eye view of the town, images of a bridge over the Yellowstone River, and the Northern Pacific Railway depot and railyard as well as views of sites and rock formations in the vicinity including horse and sheep ranches, the Badlands, Eagle Butte, Devil's Gulch, Hungary Joe, Natural Bridge, and Twin Caves; a group of three views by an unidentified photographer documents an automobile on its side and mired in mud

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A view of three grain elevators along a railway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes images of main street, a line of grain binders in a field during harvest, grain elevators, and cattle shipping. Several postcards also include correspondence, possibly from Alvina's husband, Includes a view of the W. A. Orgain Company store and other storefronts as well as views of the cattle yard and grain harvesting crews; correspondence on verso of postcards likely by Raleigh H. Welsh, some likely using a combination of his initials as a signature

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior portrait of three men, identified on recto as Floyd, Art, and Riva, with three horses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Views of a group of at least eight children, women, and men riding in a horse-drawn sleigh and sitting on a sled in a snow-covered landscape as well as a view of the group at the railroad depot

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composite image comprised of seven photographs of sites including a bird's-eye view of Hatton and buildings including Front Street, churches, grain elevators, and high school

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of storefronts, including F. P. Lippert Auto Company; correspondence on verso, likely by Levi Carl Alm to Edna Gilbert.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of Main Street that includes storefronts for the First State Bank and United States Post Office as well as a hotel and meat market with a grain elevator and herd of cattle in the background

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of a group by [No Forename] Wright that includes children and men with a sailboat on Rock Lake in the vicinity of Rocklake, Minnesota

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Group of four interrelated images that include views and informal portraits of three men at a fruit farm, water treatment center, and construction site for the Wasco County Courthouse designed by architect Charles Jesse Crandall (1858-1917) as well as two men at the Odd Fellow Cemetery and gravesite of Elizabeth Rosley Corcoran (1815-1910)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of waves from the Pacific Ocean at the seashore

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of the Citizens Bank Building on Main Street at Aberdeen by N. A. (Nicholas Arthur) Brothers (1871-1942)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Group of photographic postcards related to construction at the Bellingham Cement Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Informal exterior portrait of three men posed standing with a corn stalk, likely at a railroad depot

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of a railway bridge across the Baker River and a cement plant

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of the Skagit Valley and Cascade Range published by the M.L. Zercher Book and Stationery Company, Topeka, Kansas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a view of Main Street and storefronts published by Bradford & Co., St. Joseph, Michigan, as well as discrete views of children at the Amery High School and a city street with large snowdrifts along a sidewalk and buildings in the background that include a candy store

did.physdesc: 1 photographic postcard, 13.8 x 8.8 cm, and 1 photographic postcard, 8.8 x 13.8 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a bird's-eye view of a main street in Cameron and a view of the Norwegian Lutheran Church, later known as Faith Lutheran

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exterior views of the Scandinavian Evangelical Congregation, later known as Deronda Lutheran Church (razed October 2022), and parsonage as well as the residence of L. P. (Lars Pederson) Melbostad (1870-1949) as well as an interior of a general store

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of a tree-lined road with a church and residences

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composite images that includes groups from 1912 summer school related to teacher institute training at the Superior Normal School (later University of Wisconsin - Superior) and an excursion via the Columbia steamship on the Fond du Lac Line and railway from Superior to Fond du Lac, Wisconsin; correspondence on verso by Virgil Everett McCaskill (1866-1922), then president of the Superior Normal School, on June 7, 1913, inquires if Welsh will be joining the summer school of 1913

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of a bridge over the St. Croix River at Hudson, Wisconsin, with a man visible in the foreground

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of Main Street that includes the Blumenstein Opera House built in 1910 by Aaron Blumenstein (1865-1950)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of the Modern Woodmen of America hall, originally built in 1896 as an opera hall and roller rink, but sold the same year to the fraternal benefit society, which operated the building as a meeting space until 1945, when it became an Allis-Chalmers Agency

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of water discharge from the Cascade Mill and Dam on the South Fork Kinnickinnic River at Glen Park; in 1925, the Minneapolis Bridge Company constructed a suspension bridge at the site

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images include discrete views of Cedar Lake and Star Prairie as well as a composite image that combines views of Cedar Lake, dam on Cedar Creek at Huntington, and Star Prairie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Views of the Superior Normal School (later University of Wisconsin - Superior) that include Old Main building and the dormitory, Crownhart Hall, where Welsh resided while enrolled in a summer session at the school
